clean_text: Alhamdulillah. Continuing where we left off with regards to the story of the prophet sallall.aii wasallam's first reception of revelation from Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. Revelation is a very strenuous process. It is not easy. It's not simple. It has physiological effects and it has emotional and psychological stresses that are involved in the process. We are speaking about the words of Allah Almighty, the divine, the sublime, the endless, the infinite, the unique. He is taking his words and putting them into the heart of a human being. This is not a simple process. Allah Subhanahu Waaala says to the prophet Sam, he said indeed we are going to send down upon you a very heavy and lofty and grave speech and revelation. Notice the words that were used here. In the Arabic language can be used to articulate something that is physically heavy. Can be used to articulate some or to describe something that is grave and weighty. And both are intended here in this verse. Zabat and Bkari said that the prophet sallall.aii wasallam received revelation while I was sitting next to him. Zenit is one of the scribes of the prophet sallall.aii wasallam. when revelation would come or they were anticipating revelation, he would call for zade and summon him. He would come, he would bring his pen, he would bring his slates, whatever they were, and he would wait to write whatever new verses the prophet sallall.aii wasallam recites to him. And so as they were waiting, he was sitting next to the prophet sallallah.aii wasallam this sitting next to each other a kimbo. And he said the prophet sallall.aii wasallam's thigh was on my thigh. Right? If you could imagine that we're sitting cross-legged next to each other. His thigh was on mine. They were very close to each other. He said when revelation suddenly came. He said and I felt the physical weight of his body become heavy. So heavy that I thought my thigh was going to smash. It was going to burst from the weight of his thigh just sitting on mine. From the revelation in a sunin it's mentioned that the prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam was riding on his camel in Mecca when he received the chapter of al- Mida. Al- Mida is one of the last chapters of the Quran to be revealed to the prophet sallall.aii wasallam. It was revealed to him in Mecca during he was on his camel and he was being led during when was revealed to him. The companion said that the camel started to buckle under the weight of the revelation. It almost collapsed under the weight of the revelation. The prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam had to get down off of the camel before it broke the camel's legs because of the weight. This is a physical weight that these words would bring. Not to mention the gravity of the words of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. And so Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala says And so this revelation is not easy. The prophet sallallah.aii wasallam had to have a preface to prepare his heart for this revelation. That preparation came in the form of a few months. Some scholars posit that it is about 6 months of premonitions that he would have in his sleep. Sallallahu alaihi wasallam. He'd wake up and he would see exactly vividly, word for word, movement for movement, action for action, moment for moment, what he saw in his premonition, in his sleep play out in the real world, in his wake. summary: This lecture discusses the story of Prophet Muhammad's first revelation from Allah, focusing on the physical and spiritual nature of receiving divine revelation. The speaker explains that revelation is a strenuous process with physiological effects, citing examples from hadith where companions witnessed the physical weight of revelation—such as the camel buckling under the weight when the Prophet received Surah Al-Ma'idah. The lecture details the preparation period of approximately six months of premonitions in sleep that preceded the first revelation, and describes the Prophet's practice of contemplation and solitude in the cave of Hira during Ramadan, where he would reflect on existence, purpose, and the truth of Allah's religion.
suggested_title: The Story of the Prophet's First Revelation: The Meeting with Angel Gabriel in the Cave of Hira
catchy_title: Why Revelation Is Harder Than You Think
